  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Backend Engineer",
  "description": "Vaste Holdings is building a regulated investment and savings platform for the Nigerian retail market &mdash; enabling individuals to onboard, fund a wallet, and access structured investment products through a secure, compliant digital experience.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Backend Engineer

Job Location:&nbsp;&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a Backend Engineer with strong experience in fintech and asset management processes to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will help build and maintain backend systems that support investment portfolios, client fund management, and reporting for asset managers.
	The role requires strong expertise in Java Spring Boot for backend development, as well as the ability to build web interfaces using React and Thymeleaf (or similar MVC libraries).


Key Responsibilities


	Build REST APIs and microservices using Java Spring Boot.
	Develop web dashboards and admin interfaces using React and Thymeleaf/MVC frameworks.
	Support core asset management workflows, including portfolio tracking, subscriptions/redemptions, and fee calculations.
	Integrate systems with market data providers, custody platforms, and core banking systems.
	Ensure security, audit logging, scalability, and performance of financial data systems.
	Collaborate with portfolio managers, quantitative analysts, and operations teams.


Required Experience &amp; Skills
Technical Skills:


	Minimum of five (5) years of hands-on backend development experience.
	Strong expertise in Java and Spring Boot (Spring Data, Spring Security, MVC).
	Working knowledge of React (Hooks, Components, State Management).
	Experience with Thymeleaf or server-side templating libraries such as JSP, Mustache, or FreeMarker.
	Strong understanding of REST APIs, JPA/Hibernate, and SQL databases (PostgreSQL/MySQL).
	Familiarity with Git, Maven/Gradle, and unit testing frameworks such as JUnit and Mockito.


Cloud &amp; Infrastructure:


	Experience with cloud platforms such as Heroku, AWS, or Azure.
	Familiarity with Kafka, Redis, Docker, and Kubernetes.
	Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced but supportive fintech environment.


Fintech &amp; Regulatory Experience:
We place strong emphasis on prior experience within fintech and asset management environments. Candidates should have:


	Working knowledge of Nigerian fintech regulatory requirements, including:
	
		CBN digital wallet regulations
		SEC Nigeria investment product requirements
		NDPR data protection obligations
		AML/CFT compliance standards
	
	
	Understanding of financial instruments such as:
	
		Equities
		Fixed income instruments
		Mutual funds
		ETFs
	
	


Preferred Industry Experience:
Ideal candidates should have worked on one or more of the following:


	Portfolio management systems
	Investment or wealth management platforms
	Trading or order management systems
	Fund accounting or NAV processing engines


Asset Management Process Knowledge:
Candidates should also be familiar with:


	Client onboarding and KYC integration
	Subscription and redemption workflows
	Management fee and performance fee structures
	Corporate actions (dividends, bonuses, share splits)
	Real-time P&amp;L and portfolio valuation


Salary
N600,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a short note covering their most relevant fintech delivery experience, where available, references from previous technical leadership engagements are strongly encouraged to:&nbsp;careers@vastefinance.com&nbsp;or&nbsp;matthew.adesina@vasteltd.comusing the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Backend Engineer Job at Vaste Holdings




        
    

        
        Vaste Holdings is building a regulated investment and savings platform for the Nigerian retail market — enabling individuals to onboard, fund a wallet, and access structured investment products through a secure, compliant digital experience.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Backend Engineer

Job Location:  Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a Backend Engineer with strong experience in fintech and asset management processes to join our team.
	The ideal candidate will help build and maintain backend systems that support investment portfolios, client fund management, and reporting for asset managers.
	The role requires strong expertise in Java Spring Boot for backend development, as well as the ability to build web interfaces using React and Thymeleaf (or similar MVC libraries).


Key Responsibilities


	Build REST APIs and microservices using Java Spring Boot.
	Develop web dashboards and admin interfaces using React and Thymeleaf/MVC frameworks.
	Support core asset management workflows, including portfolio tracking, subscriptions/redemptions, and fee calculations.
	Integrate systems with market data providers, custody platforms, and core banking systems.
	Ensure security, audit logging, scalability, and performance of financial data systems.
	Collaborate with portfolio managers, quantitative analysts, and operations teams.


Required Experience & Skills
Technical Skills:


	Minimum of five (5) years of hands-on backend development experience.
	Strong expertise in Java and Spring Boot (Spring Data, Spring Security, MVC).
	Working knowledge of React (Hooks, Components, State Management).
	Experience with Thymeleaf or server-side templating libraries such as JSP, Mustache, or FreeMarker.
	Strong understanding of REST APIs, JPA/Hibernate, and SQL databases (PostgreSQL/MySQL).
	Familiarity with Git, Maven/Gradle, and unit testing frameworks such as JUnit and Mockito.


Cloud & Infrastructure:


	Experience with cloud platforms such as Heroku, AWS, or Azure.
	Familiarity with Kafka, Redis, Docker, and Kubernetes.
	Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced but supportive fintech environment.


Fintech & Regulatory Experience:
We place strong emphasis on prior experience within fintech and asset management environments. Candidates should have:


	Working knowledge of Nigerian fintech regulatory requirements, including:
	
		CBN digital wallet regulations
		SEC Nigeria investment product requirements
		NDPR data protection obligations
		AML/CFT compliance standards
	
	
	Understanding of financial instruments such as:
	
		Equities
		Fixed income instruments
		Mutual funds
		ETFs
	
	


Preferred Industry Experience:
Ideal candidates should have worked on one or more of the following:


	Portfolio management systems
	Investment or wealth management platforms
	Trading or order management systems
	Fund accounting or NAV processing engines


Asset Management Process Knowledge:
Candidates should also be familiar with:


	Client onboarding and KYC integration
	Subscription and redemption workflows
	Management fee and performance fee structures
	Corporate actions (dividends, bonuses, share splits)
	Real-time P&L and portfolio valuation


Salary
N600,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a short note covering their most relevant fintech delivery experience, where available, references from previous technical leadership engagements are strongly encouraged to: [email protected] or [email protected]using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an initial conversation with the team.

Experienced Sales Engineer Job at Adeptal Limited

At Adeptal Limited, we offer alternative and efficient diesel and oil filtration technology to all types of systems where these assets (diesel & oil) are paramount, to drive sustainability, cost savings, systems life span elongation, waste reduction, and environmental protection.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Experienced Sales Engineer

Job Location: Anthony, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary 


	We are currently seeking a experienced, qualified and result-driven professional to fill the position of Sales Engineer in our organization.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and develop new business opportunities and potential clients.
	Promote and sell the company’s products and engineering solutions.
	Prepare technical proposals, quotations, and presentations for clients.
	Conduct product demonstrations and technical consultations.
	Build and maintain long-term relationships with customers.
	Negotiate contracts and close sales deals effectively.
	Provide after-sales support and ensure customer satisfaction.
	Prepare periodic sales reports and market intelligence updates


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering or any related discipline relevant to Sales Engineering.
	2–4 years of proven experience as a Sales Engineer or in a similar technical sales role.
	Strong technical, communication, and negotiation skills.
	Ability to understand customer requirements and provide suitable technical solutions.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications.
	Strong interpersonal and relationship management skills.
	Ability to work independently and meet sales targets.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and application letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

After a very competitive bidding process, TICT emerged as the concessionaire of Terminal B, Tincan Island Port. Armed with a long-term lease agreement with the Federal Government of Nigeria, and an unwavering commitment to continued investment in Port development, we are poised to position ourselves as a key industry player within the African sub-region.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Project Coordinator

Job Location:&nbsp;Apapa, Lagos
Employment Type: Contract

Roles and Responsibilities
Project Coordination &amp; Implementation:


	Coordinate operational aspects of projects affecting terminal operations, infrastructure, equipment deployment, or systems
	Track project timelines, deliverables, and milestones to ensure timely execution
	Support project planning, implementation, testing, and operational rollout
	Ensure that operational requirements are clearly defined and integrated into project plans.


Operations Representation in Projects:


	Act as the Operations Department&rsquo;s lead representative on the full spectrum of projects with operational impact
	Provide operational insights and recommendations during project design and implementation phases
	Ensure projects align with operational workflows, safety requirements, and productivity goals
	Represent operational interests during cross-functional project meetings.


Stakeholder Coordination:


	Liaise with internal teams including Planning, Yard Operations, Ship-side Operations and other Departments
	Coordinate with external vendors, contractors, and consultants where projects involve operational components
	Facilitate communication between project teams and operational units.


Process Improvement &amp; Optimization:


	Evaluate potential operational impact of proposed projects and initiatives
	Identify risks to ongoing operations and propose mitigation measures
	Support operational readiness for new infrastructure, equipment, or systems introduced through projects
	Identify opportunities for operational improvements through projects and initiatives
	Support implementation of process optimization initiatives within terminal operations
	Monitor post-project operational performance and recommend adjustments where necessary.


Monitoring &amp; Reporting:


	Maintain project documentation, action trackers, and status reports
	Provide regular updates to Operations Management on project progress and operational implications
	Track operational KPIs affected by project implementation
	Support operational testing of new systems, equipment, or processes
	Coordinate operational training and handover during project implementation
	Ensure smooth transition from project implementation to operational integration.


Other Responsibilities:


	Perform other operations-related coordination tasks as assigned by the Line Manager.


Minimum Educational Qualifications / Certifications / Years of Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Maritime Studies, Logistics, Engineering, Business Administration, or related fields
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in port/terminal operations, logistics, or operational project coordination.
	Project management training or certification (e.g., PMP, PRINCE2) is an advantage.


Skills and Competency Requirements:


	Knowledge of project coordination and implementation processes
	Ability to analyze operational workflows and identify improvement opportunities
	Strong understanding of port/terminal operations
	Proficiency in project tracking tools and operational reporting
	Strong coordination and stakeholder management skills
	Excellent communication and presentation skills
	Strong analytical and problem solving ability
	Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously
	High attention to detail and organizational skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@tict-ng.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

After a very competitive bidding process, TICT emerged as the concessionaire of Terminal B, Tincan Island Port. 	Coordinate operational training and handover during project implementation
	Ensure smooth transition from project implementation to operational integration.

Junior Project Officer Job at Medcourt Support Services Limited (MSS)




        
    

        
        Medcourt Support Services Limited (MSS) is the premiere health-care solution company based in Lagos, Nigeria. Founded in 2013, we pride ourselves in helping you stay healthy by bringing the best of health-care solutions closer to you. Over the years, we have helped our customers both individuals and corporations-tick all the boxes of accessing quality, affordable and timely health-care solutions that they need to stay healthy, happy, and fit.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Junior Project Officer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary

	We are seeking a proactive, organized, and detail-oriented Junior Project Officer to support the planning, coordination, execution, and monitoring of projects across the organization.
	The ideal candidate will work closely with the Project Manager and cross-functional teams to ensure project activities are properly coordinated, executed efficiently, and completed within agreed timelines.
	This role is ideal for an early-career professional looking to build hands-on experience in project coordination, reporting, stakeholder communication, and operational support within a fast-paced environment.


Key Responsibilities


	Support the Project Manager in planning, coordinating, and implementing project activities.
	Assist in tracking project timelines, deliverables, and milestones.
	Coordinate project meetings, schedules, documentation, and follow-ups.
	Prepare project reports, updates, and presentations for management review.
	Liaise with vendors, clients, and internal teams to support smooth project execution.
	Monitor project progress and escalate issues where necessary.
	Ensure proper documentation and filing of project records.
	Assist with budget tracking and resource coordination.
	Support risk identification and issue resolution activities.
	Ensure compliance with company standards and project requirements.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Project Management, Mechanical Engineering, Civil Engineering, or a related field.
	0 - 2 years of experience in project coordination, project support, internship, or administrative roles.
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team environment.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, especially Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.
	Basic knowledge of project management tools or methodologies is an added advantage.
	Strong willingness to learn and grow within a project-focused role.

	Project Coordination
	Communication Skills
	Time Management
	Reporting & Documentation
	Attention to Detail
	Problem Solving
	Team Collaboration
	Organizational Skills.

  "title": "Deputy Safety Manager",
  "description": "Tincan Island Container Terminal Limited (TICT), established in 2004, is a multinational company operating in Nigeria with its Terminal located at Tin-can Island Port, Terminal B (2nd Gate), Apapa, Lagos and has its Head Office at 1/3 Point Road, Apapa, Lagos. After a very competitive bidding process, TICT emerged as the concessionaire of Terminal B, Tincan Island Port. Armed with a long-term lease agreement with the Federal Government of Nigeria, and an unwavering commitment to continued investment in Port development, we are poised to position ourselves as a key industry player within the African sub-region.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Deputy Safety Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Apapa, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Deputy Safety Manager supports the Safety Manager in implementing, monitoring, and improving the company&rsquo;s safety management systems and procedures.
	The role ensures compliance with HSE regulations, supports risk assessments, leads site inspections and investigations, and promotes a strong safety culture across terminal operations.
	The Deputy Safety Manager acts as a bridge between the safety team, operations staff, and contractors, helping to ensure that day-to-day activities meet safety expectations.


Key Duties and Responsibilities
HSE Policy Implementation &amp; Monitoring:


	Assist in enforcing compliance with TICT&rsquo;s safety policies, procedures, and local/international safety regulations
	Support the execution of safety strategies and initiatives aligned with terminal operations
	Participate in the regular review and update of safety policies and risk control procedures
	Monitor the availability and condition of emergency equipment and PPE


Risk Assessment &amp; Incident Management:


	Support the coordination of hazard identification, risk assessments (HIRA), and job safety analyses (JSA)
	Investigate incidents, near misses, and unsafe conditions; assist in root cause analysis and ensure corrective actions are implemented
	Maintain and update incident registers, investigation reports, and follow-up logs
	Conduct routine and ad-hoc safety inspections of terminal operations, equipment, and work environments
	Participate in internal and external audits; track and support the closure of corrective actions
	Ensure compliance with statutory requirements and safety audit standards


Training &amp; Safety Awareness:


	Support the planning and delivery of safety induction, toolbox talks, and refresher training programs for staff and contractors
	Promote safety campaigns, awareness initiatives, and weekly HSE engagement meetings
	Assist in developing and maintaining emergency response plans and procedures
	Participate in emergency drills and ensure response readiness across teams
	Maintain up-to-date training records and develop safety communication materials
	Provide feedback and support for the development of safety programs and project


Contractor &amp; Compliance Monitoring:


	Ensure contractors and third parties adhere to TICT&rsquo;s safety standards and operating procedures
	Review and verify contractor safety plans, permits-to-work, and compliance documentation


Monitoring &amp; Reporting:


	Prepare and submit safety performance reports, incident summaries, and KPI dashboards
	Maintain documentation for regulatory compliance and audit readiness
	Provide input for monthly and quarterly safety review meetings


Other Responsibilities:


	Perform other duties as assigned by the Line Manager


Minimum Educational Qualifications / Certifications / Years of Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Environmental Science, Engineering, Occupational Health &amp; Safety, or a related field.
	5 - 7 years of experience in HSE experience, with at least 2 years in a supervisory or leadership role.
	NEBOSH International General Certificate (required).
	ISO 45001 Lead Auditor, HSE Level 1-3, or equivalent (preferred)
	Membership in relevant professional bodies (e.g., ISPON, IOSH, etc.) is an advantage.
	Prior experience in ports, terminals, logistics, oil &amp; gas, or manufacturing environments is highly desirable.


Skills and Competency Requirements:


	Strong understanding of HSE laws, regulations, and industry standards (e.g., ISO 45001, OSHA).
	Knowledge of terminal operations, equipment safety, and contractor management.
	Familiarity with environmental management and emergency response procedures.
	Proficiency in HSE reporting tools and Microsoft Office Suite.
	Ability to conduct safety inspections, audits, risk assessments, and investigations.
	Strongpresentationand training facilitation skills.
	Ability to influence safety culture and supervise HSE personnel.
	Rootcause analysisand problem-solving.
	Clear and effective written and verbal communication.
	Accurate documentation and compliance tracking.
	Takes initiative in identifying and mitigating risks.


Total Compensation
Attractive.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@tict-ng.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",
